message:
fix: handle transient WriteZero errors in SSE streaming gracefully (closes #12)

Some providers (e.g. Kimi K2.5) send empty SSE frames that surface as
std::io::ErrorKind::WriteZero. Previously these were treated as fatal,
killing the agent turn. Now both the Chat Completions and Responses API
stream loops detect WriteZero specifically, log a warning via tracing,
and continue reading. A consecutive-error cap (5) prevents infinite
loops if the condition persists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
